prompt: Design a small but defensible experiment that tests whether explicit committee orchestration improves research-style reasoning beyond gains that come only from extra inference compute.
context: Assume you are writing the experimental design section of an academic paper. The same base model is available in both the committee system and the single-model baseline. The goal is to separate coordination effects from raw compute effects.
objectives: Define a treatment condition and at least one baseline
Explain how total inference budget is matched fairly
Specify task families, metrics, and failure analysis
constraints: Keep the design realistic for a small lab
Avoid relying on proprietary internal metrics
deliverable: A concise research design note suitable for a methods section.
evaluation_criteria: Calls out at least one confounder
